alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same replication and extension claim but reports it with higher certainty than the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Agnostic Visualization Recommendation Systems (VRSs) aim to autonomously identify relevant relationships in data and make design choices for insightful visualizations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Agnostic Visualization Recommendation Systems (VRSs) aim to autonomously identify relevant relationships in data and make design choices for insightful visualizations.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this contribution statement verbatim, with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The progress of agnostic VRSs is hindered by the absence of standardized datasets for training recommendation algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The progress of agnostic VRSs is hindered by the absence of standardized datasets for training recommendation algorithms.
alignments.rationale: This limitation is carried forward unchanged from preprint to publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Agnostic VRSs face challenges in learning design rules and defining quantitative criteria for evaluating the effectiveness of generated plots.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Agnostic VRSs face challenges in learning design rules and defining quantitative criteria for evaluating the perceptual effectiveness of generated plots.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the qualifier 'perceptual', narrowing the evaluation focus to perceptual effectiveness while otherwise preserving the limitation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This paper summarizes the literature on agnostic VRSs and outlines promising future research directions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This article summarizes the literature on agnostic VRSs and outlines promising future research directions.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the summary and future-directions claim with equivalent meaning; only minor wording changes appear.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using a language-model-powered writing assistant affected the opinions expressed in participants' writing.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using a language-model-powered writing assistant affected the opinions expressed in participants' writing.
alignments.rationale: The result is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The language model shifted participants' opinions in the subsequent attitude survey.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The language model shifted participants' opinions in the subsequent attitude survey.
alignments.rationale: This finding is carried forward unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The opinions built into AI language technologies need to be monitored and engineered more carefully.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The opinions built into AI language technologies need to be monitored and engineered more carefully.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about monitoring and engineering model opinions is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Magnitude-based pruning optimises deep learning models for edge inference.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Magnitude-based pruning optimises deep learning models for edge inference.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion verbatim with no change in scope or expressed confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We have achieved over 75% model size reduction with a higher accuracy than the original multi-output regression model for head-pose estimation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We have achieved over 75% model size reduction with a higher accuracy than the original multi-output regression model for head-pose estimation.
alignments.rationale: The result is restated identically in the publication with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed ordering techniques allow the models to better capture the relationships between the channel snapshots within the sequence, irrespective of the sequence length.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models effectively capture relationships within sequences of channel snapshots without increasing complexity.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claim that relationships are captured but omits the explicit "irrespective of sequence length" wording and adds a complexity-related clarification, narrowing the original scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new technique called reverse positional encoding is introduced in the transformer model to improve the robustness of the model against varying sequence lengths.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed techniques, including reverse positional encoding and reversed encoder outputs, improve the robustness of the transformer and Seq2Seq-attn models against varying sequence lengths.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly names the same reverse positional encoding technique and states the same effect on robustness with matching emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both sequence-to-sequence models with attention and transformer models are adapted from natural language processing to tackle the complex challenge of channel prediction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed techniques, including reverse positional encoding and reversed encoder outputs, improve the robustness of the transformer and Seq2Seq-attn models against varying sequence lengths.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the contribution in terms of concrete model variants (Seq2Seq-attn and transformer) and their improved robustness, which clarifies the adaptation claim though with more emphasis on robustness than on the NLP origin.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural networks can be trained without assumptions about user velocity, relying solely on noisy channel observations during training.
alignments.rationale: This publication-level claim about training without velocity assumptions does not have a direct counterpart in the preprint and appears as a new contribution.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The capability of the proposed models remains robust across varying sequence lengths, representing a substantial improvement over existing methodologies.
alignments.rationale: This higher-level concluding claim summarizing robustness and improvement is not explicitly present in the preprint and thus treated as an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed stochastic label mechanism includes identifying a correct class label from a small number of randomly given labels and annotating with a None label when the correct class label is not present.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed labeling mechanism called stochastic labels reduces the time spent browsing labeling space by annotating the instance from a small labels subset.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core mechanism (labeling from a small subset) but omits the detailed "None" annotation behavior and emphasizes practical labeling-time reduction, narrowing the original description.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach developed in this paper provides an unbiased estimator that requires less supervised information to train a multi-class classifier.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed labeling mechanism called stochastic labels reduces the time spent browsing labeling space by annotating the instance from a small labels subset.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes practical label-subset annotation and labeling-time reduction rather than explicitly claiming an unbiased estimator that needs less supervision, shifting the emphasis of the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The theoretical justification of the proposed method is supported by deriving the estimation error bound.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The empirical risk could converge to the true classification risk as the number of training samples increases.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a theoretical convergence result related to the estimator's properties; this reframes the original error-bound statement as a convergence claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiments demonstrate the superiority of the proposed method compared to existing state-of-the-art methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method surpasses state-of-the-art weakly supervised methods, highlighting its efficiency and robustness.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports empirical superiority over state-of-the-art weakly supervised methods, directly aligning with the preprint's experimental-result claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Online active learning strategies are prone to sample outliers, which degrades the predictive performance of models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The currently available query strategies in online active learning are prone to sample outliers, which degrades the predictive performance of the models.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same limitation about outliers in online active learning with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed solution, which bounds the search area of a conditional D-optimal algorithm and uses a robust estimator, improves the performance of online active learning in the presence of outliers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed solution, which bounds the search area of a conditional D-optimal algorithm and uses a robust estimator, effectively improves the performance of online active learning in the presence of outliers.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the proposed methodological solution and its effect on robustness to outliers without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study expands the potential applications of online active learning by addressing the issue of outliers.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include the broader claim about expanding applications; instead it adds a different concluding claim about operational benefits, so this specific application-expansion claim was removed.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The study demonstrates that online active learning can alleviate the computational burden and operational expenses related to labeling in high-volume production processes.
alignments.rationale: This publication-level claim about operational and cost benefits is not present in the preprint and therefore treated as a newly added conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The automated user-friendly framework, HOAX, effectively performs hyperparameter optimization for neural networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The automated user-friendly framework, HOAX, effectively performs hyperparameter optimization for neural networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the contribution verbatim with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neural network generated potential energy surfaces (PESs) reduces the computational costs compared to the ab initio-based PESs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural network-generated potential energy surfaces (PESs) reduce computational costs compared to ab initio-based PESs.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about reduced computational cost with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The resulting neural networks-based generated PESs are in reasonable agreement with the ab initio-based PESs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The resulting neural networks-based generated PESs are in reasonable agreement with the ab initio-based PESs.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the agreement between NN-generated and ab initio PESs with similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Finding optimal hyperparameters for applying machine learning algorithms remains a challenge.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Finding optimal hyperparameters for machine learning algorithms remains a challenge.
alignments.rationale: The limitation statement is carried over unchanged into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PresSim is a novel end-to-end framework that synthesizes sensor data from videos of human activities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PresSim utilizes a 3-stage process involving computer vision, physics simulation, and deep learning to generate simulated pressure sensor data.
alignments.rationale: The publication rephrases and elaborates the framework's generation process (naming specific components) rather than introducing a different claim; overall certainty is lower in the paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework significantly reduces the effort required for data collection from pressure sensors in human activity recognition.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PresSim significantly reduces the effort required for data collection from pressure sensors for human activity recognition.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution claim with effectively identical wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PresSim utilizes a 3-stage process involving 3D activity information extraction, floor mesh deformation simulation, and simulated pressure sensor data generation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PresSim utilizes a 3-stage process involving computer vision, physics simulation, and deep learning to generate simulated pressure sensor data.
alignments.rationale: The publication rewords the three stages using different technical labels (e.g., 'deep learning' vs the preprint's final stage) and expresses lower certainty, so the claim is clarified but equivalent in scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The validation of PresSim was conducted using a monocular camera and a pressure-sensing fitness mat with nine participants performing yoga sequences.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not reproduce these specific validation details (participants, monocular camera, yoga sequences) and instead presents quantitative comparison metrics not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AI-powered chatbot improved consent form reading compared to form-based interaction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AI-powered chatbot improved consent form reading compared to form-based interaction.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result claim with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The chatbot promoted participants' feelings of agency during the informed consent process.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The chatbot promoted participants’ feelings of agency during the informed consent process.
alignments.rationale: The claim is reproduced verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of the chatbot closed the power gap between the participant and the researcher.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of the chatbot closed the power gap between the participant and the researcher.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps this result claim unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The altered power dynamic introduced by the chatbot might eventually benefit study response quality.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The altered power dynamic introduced by the chatbot might eventually benefit study response quality.
alignments.rationale: The speculative conclusion is carried forward into the publication without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method is highly effective and efficient in simulation scenarios.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method is highly effective in simulation scenarios.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the effectiveness claim but drops the explicit mention of efficiency, rephrasing slightly without changing the overall scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method delivers notable performance improvements in TEM experiments compared to other material science benchmarks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method delivers notable performance improvements in TEM experiments compared to other material science benchmarks.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this experimental result claim with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: New ridge detection algorithms are defined to non-parametrically estimate explicit trajectories of atomic-level object locations as a continuous function of time.
alignments.pub_claim_text: New ridge detection algorithms are defined to non-parametrically estimate explicit trajectories of atomic-level object locations as a continuous function of time.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this methodological contribution without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Charting the sociotechnical gap in Explainable AI (XAI) improves problem understanding and provides actionable insights to enhance explainability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Charting the sociotechnical gap in Explainable AI (XAI) improves problem understanding and provides actionable insights to enhance explainability.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this high-level conclusion identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A framework has been empirically derived that facilitates systematic charting of the sociotechnical gap in XAI.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A framework has been empirically derived that facilitates systematic charting of the sociotechnical gap in XAI by connecting AI guidelines.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the description by explicitly noting the framework connects AI guidelines, clarifying its mechanism while keeping the original claim's scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework connects AI guidelines in the context of XAI and elucidates their application to address the sociotechnical gap.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A framework has been empirically derived that facilitates systematic charting of the sociotechnical gap in XAI by connecting AI guidelines.
alignments.rationale: The publication elevates this result to a higher-certainty contribution, explicitly emphasizing the connection to AI guidelines (preprint had lower certainty).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of the framework to a third case in a new domain showcases its affordances.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework has been applied to a third case in a new domain, demonstrating its affordances.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this empirical result with higher stated certainty, so the claim is unchanged but confidence is increased.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework expands the XAI design space by making conceptual and practical contributions to understanding the sociotechnical gap.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework makes conceptual and practical contributions to understanding the sociotechnical gap in XAI, thereby expanding the XAI design space.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same conceptual and practical contribution but with somewhat lower certainty than the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DualMatch effectively fuses relational and temporal information for entity alignment on temporal knowledge graphs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DualMatch effectively fuses relational and temporal information for entity alignment on temporal knowledge graphs.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this methodological contribution verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DualMatch achieves entity alignment without necessitating seed alignment through an unsupervised method.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DualMatch achieves entity alignment without necessitating seed alignment through an unsupervised method.
alignments.rationale: The result claim remains the same in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DualMatch outperforms state-of-the-art methods in terms of H@1 by 2.4% - 10.7% and MRR by 1.7% - 7.6% on three real-world temporal knowledge graph datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DualMatch significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods in entity alignment on three real-world temporal knowledge graph datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the outperforming claim but removes exact percentage ranges, presenting a more general 'significant' improvement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing studies have not thoroughly exploited the advantages of temporal information in temporal knowledge graphs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing studies have not thoroughly exploited the advantages of temporal information in temporal knowledge graphs.
alignments.rationale: The limitation/observation is preserved unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The concept of intelligent software is flawed due to the influence of hardware on software behavior. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The concept of intelligent software is flawed due to the influence of hardware on software behaviour. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The wording and meaning are effectively identical (only minor spelling difference); the certainty remains high and the conclusion unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: To make objective claims regarding performance, we must avoid computational dualism. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: To make objective claims regarding performance, we must avoid computational dualism. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim and certainty in preprint and publication; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cognition can be formalised as a part of the environment rather than as a disembodied policy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cognition is formalised as a part of the environment rather than as a disembodied policy. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim's content is the same but the publication expresses higher certainty (medium -> high), so the semantic content is unchanged while confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Objective upper bounds for intelligent behavior can be established. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Objective upper bounds for intelligent behaviour can be established. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The statement and its reported certainty remain the same between versions, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AGI will be safer, but more limited, than theorised. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: AGI will be safer, but more limited, than theorised. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion's content is identical but the publication reports higher confidence (low -> medium), so the claim is unchanged semantically but confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Knowledge Transferable Graph Neural Network (KT-GNN) effectively models distribution shifts during message passing and representation learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Knowledge Transferable Graph Neural Network (KTGNN) effectively models distribution-shifts during message passing. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the core claim about modeling distribution shifts but omits the explicit mention of 'representation learning', narrowing the scope of the original phrasing while keeping high confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method includes a domain-adapted feature completion and message passing mechanism that preserves domain differences. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The domain-adapted feature completion and message passing mechanism preserves domain differences while learning node representations. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The mechanism and purpose are the same, but the publication expresses lower certainty (high -> medium), so the claim is substantively unchanged but confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Comprehensive experiments demonstrate the superior performance of KT-GNN in real-world scenarios such as company financial risk assessment and political elections. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Comprehensive experiments demonstrate the superior performance of KTGNN in real-world scenarios such as company financial risk assessment and political elections. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental claim and reported high certainty are consistent between versions with no substantive changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing message-passing based GNNs struggle with VS-Graph due to assumptions that all nodes belong to the same domain. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: This limitation present in the preprint does not appear among the publication's claims; instead the publication adds different emphasis (see added claim), so the original limitation claim appears removed.

alignments.pub_claim_text: KTGNN learns representation by transferring knowledge from vocal nodes to silent nodes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces a specific description (vocal to silent node transfer) not present in the preprint, so this is an added claim in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Current memory-based approaches successfully alleviate the catastrophic forgetting problem by replaying examples from past tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Current memory-based approaches successfully alleviate the catastrophic forgetting problem by replaying examples from past tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical statement and certainty in both versions about replay-based memory approaches alleviating catastrophic forgetting.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method, Online Continual Learning via the Knowledge Invariant and Spread-out Properties (OCLKISP), constrains the evolution of embedding features to transfer inter-instance structural knowledge. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method, Online Continual Learning via the Knowledge Invariant and Spread-out Properties (OCLKISP), constrains the evolution of embedding features to transfer inter-instance structural knowledge. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution description is the same between preprint and publication with high certainty retained.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The experimental results demonstrate the efficacy of OCLKISP compared to state-of-the-art continual learning algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Empirical evaluations demonstrate the efficacy of OCLKISP compared to state-of-the-art continual learning algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental claim is substantively identical and retains high certainty in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Learning bias between current and prior tasks is an urgent problem that needs to be addressed in continual learning. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method alleviates forgetting due to learning bias while transferring structural knowledge from previous tasks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint framed learning bias as a problem; the publication instead asserts the proposed method alleviates forgetting caused by that bias, shifting from problem statement to method-specific conclusion (a revision and narrowing).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new method for disentanglement effectively disentangles the factors of variation in images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new method for disentanglement effectively disentangles the factors of variation in images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is repeated verbatim with the same certainty and result status.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model performs precise interventions on high-level semantic attributes of an image without affecting its quality. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model performs precise interventions on high-level semantic attributes of an image without affecting its quality. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method can handle imbalanced data distributions while maintaining performance. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method can handle imbalanced data distributions while maintaining performance. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim about robustness to imbalance is preserved without change in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new task of action retrieval is introduced, which involves finding the action responsible for the transition between two images. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new task of action retrieval is introduced, which involves finding the action responsible for the transition between two images. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution introducing action retrieval remains unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The data2vec-distilled representations outperformed the DINO counterpart in vehicle classification tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The data2vec-distilled representations outperformed the DINO counterpart in vehicle classification tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Exact match in claim and reported certainty between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A Top-1 classification accuracy of 97.2% was achieved for classifying 13 vehicle classes defined by the Federal Highway Administration. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A Top-1 classification accuracy of 97.2% was achieved for classifying 13 vehicle classes defined by the Federal Highway Administration. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: EvoCraft is an API that enables the programmatic generation of structures in Minecraft. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Fitness-based evolution can occasionally produce flying machines in Minecraft. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion about energy-efficiency potential but adds the qualifier "with flexible structures," which clarifies the context without changing overall confidence level.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments show that the trained models can achieve competitive results on datasets such as MNIST, CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and CIFAR10-DVS despite constraints.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments show that the trained models can achieve competitive results on datasets such as MNIST, CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and CIFAR10-DVS despite constraints.
alignments.rationale: Experimental result claim is reproduced verbatim in the publication with the same datasets and conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Scopeformer architecture is scalable and modular, allowing for the utilization of various CNN architectures as the backbone.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not restate the architecture's scalability/modularity or explicit support for various CNN backbones; this preprint claim does not appear in the publication summary.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiments show that the model performance is proportional to the number of convolutional blocks employed in the feature extractor.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model performance is proportional to the number of convolutional blocks employed in the feature extractor.
alignments.rationale: Direct match: both assert proportionality between performance and number of convolutional blocks.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best Efficient Scopeformer model achieved an accuracy of 96.94% and a weighted logarithmic loss of 0.083 with an eight times reduction in the number of trainable parameters compared to the base Scopeformer.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best Efficient Scopeformer model achieved an accuracy of 96.94% with a significant reduction in the number of trainable parameters.
alignments.rationale: Publication repeats the key accuracy result and notes a significant parameter reduction but omits the specific weighted-log-loss and the explicit "eight times" phrasing, making the published claim slightly abbreviated/clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Another version of the Efficient Scopeformer model further reduced the parameter space by almost 17 times with negligible performance reduction.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the separate "17x" parameter-reduction variant or the "negligible performance reduction" detail, so this specific claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hybrid CNNs and ViTs might provide the desired feature richness for developing accurate medical computer vision models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Hybrid architectures consisting of CNNs and ViTs provide the desired feature richness for developing accurate medical computer vision models.
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the tentative "might provide" to a firmer assertion that hybrids provide the desired feature richness and raises certainty from low to medium.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed gradient flow method can generate new samples that are close to the target dataset from the existing source dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed gradient flow method can generate new samples that are close to the target dataset from the existing source dataset.
alignments.rationale: Identical statements about the method's ability to generate target-like samples.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The gradient flow method minimizes the maximum mean discrepancy loss in the space of probability distributions on the feature-Gaussian manifold.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The gradient flow method minimizes the maximum mean discrepancy loss in the space of probability distributions on the feature-Gaussian manifold.
alignments.rationale: Direct match: same technical claim and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed discretized flow guarantees global convergence to the optimum.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed discretized flow guarantees global convergence to the optimum.
alignments.rationale: Publication repeats the same convergence guarantee and conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The gradient flow method improves the accuracy of classification models in transfer learning settings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The gradient flow method improves the accuracy of classification models in transfer learning settings.
alignments.rationale: Experimental/empirical claim reproduced unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methodology utilizes finite Gaussian Mixture Models (GMMs) to model spatial point cloud data with an adaptive number of components based on scene complexity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed methodology utilizes finite Gaussian Mixture Models (GMMs) to model spatial point cloud data with an adaptive number of components based on scene complexity.
alignments.rationale: Exact match: same methodological contribution described in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing mapping approaches require tuning parameters for specific use cases and do not generalize across diverse environments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing mapping approaches require tuning parameters for specific use cases and do not generalize across diverse environments.
alignments.rationale: Limitation/observation reproduced verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The self-organizing principle from information-theoretic learning is used to automatically adapt the complexity of the GMM model based on relevant information in the sensor data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The self-organizing principle from information-theoretic learning is used to automatically adapt the complexity of the GMM model based on relevant information in the sensor data.
alignments.rationale: Same explanatory/technical claim appears unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach is evaluated against existing point cloud modeling techniques on real-world data with varying degrees of scene complexity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach is evaluated against existing point cloud modeling techniques on real-world data with varying degrees of scene complexity.
alignments.rationale: Evaluation/result claim carried over without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed neural network architecture converts multi-fidelity modeling into a nonlinear manifold learning problem.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed neural network architecture converts multi-fidelity modeling into a nonlinear manifold learning problem.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim verbatim, so the meaning, scope and confidence are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neural network inversely learns non-trivial biases of low-fidelity sources in an interpretable and visualizable manifold.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The neural network architecture learns non-trivial biases of low-fidelity sources in an interpretable and visualizable manifold.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result with equivalent wording and intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The probabilistic manifold quantifies model form uncertainties by encoding low-fidelity sources with small bias close to the high-fidelity source.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The probabilistic manifold quantifies model form uncertainties by encoding low-fidelity sources with small bias close to the high-fidelity source.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same technical claim about the probabilistic manifold and its role in quantifying model-form uncertainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The output of the neural network is endowed with a parametric distribution to quantify aleatoric uncertainties and improve robustness and accuracy on unseen high-fidelity data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The output of the neural network is endowed with a parametric distribution to quantify aleatoric uncertainties and improve robustness and accuracy on unseen high-fidelity data.
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the same claim about parametric outputs for aleatoric uncertainty and improved robustness/accuracy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach demonstrates high predictive power while quantifying various sources of uncertainties through analytic and engineering examples.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include an explicit overarching claim about demonstrated high predictive power across analytic/engineering examples, so this preprint claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning models can be used to tackle the growing number of cyber-attack variations targeting IoT systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning models can be used to tackle the growing number of cyber-attack variations targeting IoT systems.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this high-level conclusion unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methodology was used to evaluate three supervised algorithms and one unsupervised algorithm against adversarial evasion attacks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed methodology for adversarial robustness analysis was used to evaluate three supervised algorithms and one unsupervised algorithm.
alignments.rationale: The publication rephrases the methodology claim but preserves its scope and intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: XGB consistently achieved the highest accuracy in multi-class classification despite the inherent susceptibility of tree-based algorithms to adversarial evasion attacks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: XGB consistently achieved the highest accuracy in multi-class classification despite the inherent susceptibility of tree-based algorithms to adversarial evasion attacks.
alignments.rationale: The result about XGB is repeated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results demonstrate the benefits of adversarial training and a security by design approach for a more robust IoT network intrusion detection and cyber-attack classification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Adversarial training and a security-by-design approach provide benefits for a more robust IoT network intrusion detection and cyber-attack classification.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same conclusion but reports it with reduced certainty (medium vs high), so the claim's confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The attribute-guided multi-level attention network (AG-MAN) enhances the pre-trained feature extractor to capture multi-level image embedding.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The attribute-guided multi-level attention network (AG-MAN) enhances the pre-trained feature extractor to capture multi-level image embedding.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this contribution identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AG-MAN categorizes images with the same attribute into the same class, which alleviates the feature gap problem.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AG-MAN categorizes images with the same attribute into the same class, which alleviates the feature gap problem.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same conclusion about attribute-based categorization and feature-gap alleviation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model outperforms existing attention-based methods on the FashionAI, DeepFashion, and Zappos50k datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model consistently outperforms existing attention-based methods on the FashionAI, DeepFashion, and Zappos50k datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the wording by adding 'consistently', clarifying the nature of the outperforming but not changing dataset scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AG-MAN improves the ASENet_V2 model by 2.12%, 0.31%, and 0.78% points in FashionAI, DeepFashion, and Zappos50k datasets, respectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AG-MAN improves the ASENet_V2 model by 2.12%, 0.31%, and 0.78% points in FashionAI, DeepFashion, and Zappos50k datasets, respectively.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same quantitative improvements with identical figures.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The EuclidNet framework demonstrates an average accuracy of 75% on the problem set of Euclidea without prior knowledge.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides a concrete performance metric on a specific benchmark (Euclidea), which clarifies the general effectiveness claim but narrows its scope to that dataset.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: EuclidNet utilizes the Mask R-CNN architecture to extract visual features from geometric configurations.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not mention Mask R-CNN; the specific architectural detail from the preprint is omitted in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework generates intermediary data models that refine the construction step sequence through feedback.
alignments.pub_claim_text: EuclidNet effectively utilizes backtracking for deep visual reasoning in solving complex geometry problems.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes backtracking as the mechanism for refining solution steps rather than the preprint's framing of intermediary data models and feedback, indicating a revision in the described method.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: EuclidNet is validated on complex Japanese Sangaku geometry problems, showcasing its effectiveness.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework is capable of addressing complex Japanese Sangaku geometry problems.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the validation claim but states it with slightly lower certainty (medium), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Compression is neither necessary nor sufficient to maximise performance in terms of the probability of a hypothesis generalising.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Compression is neither necessary nor sufficient to maximise performance in terms of the probability of a hypothesis generalising.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this core result verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Weakness maximisation outperforms any proxy related to length or simplicity in uniformly distributed tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is no choice of proxy that performs at least as well as weakness maximisation in all tasks while performing strictly better in at least one.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint's claim in a more formal, general logical form that clarifies its universal and comparative nature.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In experiments, maximum weakness generalised at between 1.1 and 5 times the rate of minimum description length in the context of binary arithmetic.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Weakness is a far better proxy for generalisation than compression.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this concluding statement without substantive change.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The effectiveness of Deepmind’s Apperception Engine in generalising can be explained by the use of weakness as a proxy.
alignments.rationale: This explanatory claim about DeepMind's Apperception Engine does not appear in the preprint and is newly introduced in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The end-to-end network pays attention to a set of joints specific to each action, whose encoded spatio-temporal features are aggregated to recognize the action. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed topic detection framework significantly improves topic detection compared to other methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
